description: Scratchpad v2 sweep: adds try_both (1-ply engine lookahead - tests both branches of one edge, auto-applies forced edges), same 4 puzzles, n=1, full sweep in one window.
motivation: Replace ineffective manual frames with engine-level 1-ply lookahead and see if it extends the ceiling or improves the cost profile.
formal_results: 001 solved $1.22/20 (-19% vs v1, try_both adopted unprompted); 002 solved $4.76/63 (+85% vs v1 - try_both overused where propagation suffices); 004 unsolved but clean bail $3.18/62 (-85% vs v1's $21.17, 0 assume calls); 003 clean $0.07/1.
results_description: try_both is discoverable and cleanly replaces frames; it saves a lot on failure cases via an honest early-stop signal but is over-applied on medium puzzles, and 1-ply is still too shallow for 15x15.
conclusions: Engine 1-ply lookahead improves the failure-cost profile and is self-adopted, but does not break the ceiling (>=2-ply would cross into solver territory and erase the experimental signal).
